message:
fix(fcp-sandbox): enforce ip_allow on every DNS-resolved IP (br-45tjy)

crates/fcp-sandbox/src/egress.rs:736-758 — validate_dns_resolution
previously enforced only dns_max_ips + check_ip_constraints on each
resolved IP. When host_allow permitted a hostname (e.g.
api.example.com) and ip_allow pinned a specific public IP (e.g.
93.184.216.34), a resolver returning a DIFFERENT public IP — one
that is not localhost, not private, not tailnet, not CIDR-denied —
would slip through: the explicit ip_allow pin was never consulted
after DNS resolution. That is an egress escape.

Fix: when constraints.ip_allow is non-empty, EVERY resolved IP must
be a member of the allow-list after normalization. The check runs
after check_ip_constraints so private/tailnet/cidr_deny still fail
first with their specific codes. Empty ip_allow stays open-by-default
(no regression on the common config).

Added DenyReason::IpNotAllowed so callers / audit sinks can
distinguish "unlisted resolution" from "cidr deny matched" etc.

Added ip_allow_contains() helper that normalizes IPv4-mapped IPv6
(::ffff:x.x.x.x) on BOTH the resolved IP and the allow-list entries,
so:
  * an attacker-controlled resolver cannot bypass an IPv4 allow-list
    by returning an AAAA mapped record for a different address;
  * a v6-configured allow-list correctly matches a v4 resolution of
    the same host.

## Regression tests (6 new)

  validate_dns_resolution_rejects_public_ip_outside_ip_allow
    (pins the exact escape scenario from the bead)
  validate_dns_resolution_accepts_every_ip_when_ip_allow_is_empty
    (no regression on the empty-allow-list default)
  validate_dns_resolution_accepts_only_listed_ips
  validate_dns_resolution_rejects_ipv4_mapped_ipv6_outside_ip_allow
  validate_dns_resolution_accepts_ipv4_mapped_ipv6_of_listed_ipv4
  ip_allow_contains_normalizes_ipv4_mapped_v6_in_allow_list

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-sandbox --lib
  -> 563 passed; 0 failed
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline \
    -p fcp-sandbox --lib validate_dns_resolution
  -> 16 passed (was 10 + 6 new); 0 failed

Pre-existing wasi hostcalls test failure on macOS tmp-symlink is
unrelated and reproduces on HEAD without these changes.

Closes flywheel_connectors-45tjy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
